verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is the only multi-pass transmembrane protein in the core autophagy machinery. Its dominant steady-state localization is the trans-Golgi network and a peripheral pool of ATG9A vesicles that traffic between Golgi, endosomes, and autophagosome assembly sites. However, ATG9A has a documented minority plasma membrane pool: it cycles between the PM and endosomes via clathrin-mediated endocytosis (AP2-dependent), with surface biotinylation and antibody-feeding experiments on intact cells directly detecting a surface fraction. As a multi-pass TM protein with extracellular/luminal loops, the surface pool is in principle accessible to extracellular binders. Best fit is dual_localization (vesicular cycling between endomembrane system and PM with its own TM anchor).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface pool is small and transient relative to the dominant TGN/endosomal pool; unclear whether the exposed loops present a tractable epitope for surface-directed binders.
